name: Wabi Sabi
framing: A universal iPhone case for MUJI — beauty in serenity, simplicity and imperfection.

spec.Brief: design a product MUJI could sell
spec.Concept: Wabi Sabi — beauty in serenity, simplicity and imperfection

blocks.body: An ambitious early project, and my introduction to 3D modelling. The brief was to design something MUJI could sell — the brand known for its minimalist, "this will do" approach.

We named the project Wabi Sabi, after the Japanese principle of finding beauty in serenity, simplicity and imperfection — and worked toward an object honest in form and useful in function: a universal phone case.

Using a flexible 3D-printing filament, we developed a universal case that could expand and contract to fit every iPhone from the SE to the 14 — the most recent model at the time. The flexible material doubled as protection, which became a design feature on its own: padded corners, the only addition we allowed ourselves on top of the bare case form.

blocks.alt: Iteration shots from the print runs, November 2022

blocks.body: Many iterations later we had a functional prototype that fit the whole range — kept deliberately minimal, made from one material, with one functional addition.

blocks.alt: The finished case across the phone range
